description_long: This is a weathered, industrial-style metal locker cabinet with three vertical compartments, each originally fitted with a vented door. The surface is painted in a faded, chipped green with visible rust and corrosion, especially along the edges and around the vents. Two of the doors are partially or fully detached, one lying on the ground in front, revealing the empty interior of the central compartment. The cabinet is constructed from thick sheet metal panels, with reinforced corners and a utilitarian, robust design typical of storage found in garages, workshops, or urban utility spaces. The vent slats on each door provide ventilation, and the absence of handles suggests they may have been broken off or were minimalistic in design. The overall appearance is rugged and utilitarian, with significant signs of age and use.
description: A heavily weathered green metal locker cabinet with three vented doors, two of which are detached and one lying on the ground, revealing an empty, rusted interior. The cabinet shows extensive rust, chipped paint, and a utilitarian industrial design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the cabinet's depth and the thickness of the metal panels, with one door partially open and another lying flat on the ground.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying the solid, unbroken back panel with visible rust and paint wear, and a detached door extending outward.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the cabinet's rectangular profile and the detached door leaning away from the structure.
description_view_3: Frontal view showing all three compartments, two doors open or detached, and one door completely removed and lying in front of the cabinet.
